metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4619
metadata.comment:
Rationale: "we here shown that NECAB2 immunoreactivity was distributed in the cytoplasm of striatal neurons, and never labeled the nucleus of the cell (Fig. 6A), as previously demonstrated by light microscopy (Figs. 5B and C).

Immunoreactivity for NECAB2 was also found both at postsynaptic sites, filling dendritic shafts and spines (Figs. 6B and C), and at presynaptic sites, filling axon terminals (Fig. 6D).

Using the pre-embedding immunogold method, immunoparticles labeling NECAB2 were observed in the cytoplasm, dendritic shafts and spines of striatal neurons, either associated to the endoplasmic reticulum and/or intracellular vesicles, but also showing a cytosolic pattern with no apparent association with those organelles (Figs. 6E, F and G).

Furthermore, immunoparticles were found associated to the extrasynaptic plasma membrane of dendritic spines and shafts, as well as to axon terminals at presynaptic sites (Figs. 6H, I and J). This precise distribution of NECAB2 in striatal neurons resembles that described for adenosine A2A receptor (Ciruela et al., 2006)." PMID:16481441. Experimental description: "Neuronal striatal primary cultures were obtained as described previously (Chan et al., 2001, Ciruela et al., 2001) and plated at a density of 5 × 104 cells/cm2. Membrane suspensions from rat striatum or from transfected HEK cells were obtained as described previously (Burgueño et al., 2003a, Burgueño et al., 2003b)."

"A rabbit anti-NECAB polyclonal antibody was raised by immunization of rabbits with GST-NECAB2(77-386) fusion protein following affinity purification of the antibody, as described for the generation of the rabbit anti-CTA2AR polyclonal antibody (Ciruela et al., 2004). "
